articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Saka scores stunner as England ease to win over Wales
article.description: Bukayo Saka's stunner wrapped up England's 3-0 comprehensive win against Wales as Thomas Tuchel's side built on last month's impressive victory in Serbia.
article.linkText: Saka nets stunner as England ease to Wales win

images.name: Bellamy reflects on heavy 3-0 defeat to England
images.caption: Wales boss Craig Bellamy wants his team to learn from their 3-0 defeat to England at Wembley.

video.headline: Bellamy reflects on heavy 3-0 defeat to England
video.caption: Wales boss Craig Bellamy wants his team to learn from their 3-0 defeat to England at Wembley.
video.title: Bellamy reflects on heavy 3-0 defeat to England
video.description: Wales boss Craig Bellamy wants his team to learn from their 3-0 defeat to England at Wembley.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (Bellamy reflects on heavy 3-0 defeat to England) GLOBAL 20251010

article.story:
Bukayo Saka's stunner wrapped up England's 3-0 comprehensive win against Wales as Thomas Tuchel's side built on last month's impressive victory in Serbia.

The German head coach sparked debate by leaving out star names such as Jude Bellingham and Phil Foden to stick with the squad that ended September's camp with a five-star display in Belgrade.

Tuchel's decision paid dividends as England started where they left off in Serbia, with Morgan Rogers, Ollie Watkins and Saka goals meaning Wales were down for the count just 20 minutes into Thursday's Wembley friendly.

Craig Bellamy said in the build-up that a boxing promoter would not match the team fourth in FIFA's world rankings against his 30th-placed side and Watkins should have added a fourth before half-time.

The striker, who started with skipper Harry Kane out injured, collided with a post as he attempted to score and had to be replaced at the break.

Wales can take heart from an improved second-half display as Bellamy's men turn their attention to Monday's crunch World Cup qualifier against Belgium.

A trip to Latvia, and perhaps early qualification, awaits Tuchel's side after England won their eighth successive meeting with Wales.

A sold-out crowd witnessed a powerful moment before kick-off as the players emerged from the tunnel alongside fans with dementia as part of the partnership between the Alzheimer's Society and Football Association.

Declan Rice led out England in Kane's absence and Rogers started as number 10.

The Aston Villa midfielder cut back for Antony Gordon to force Karl Darlow into a smart save before opening his England account 130 seconds into the game.

John Stones flicked on a corner that Marc Guéhi brilliantly kept alive and pulled back for Rogers to score from six yards.

Ezri Konsa saw penalty appeals fall on deaf ears, but England were not to be denied for long.

Just 11 minutes were on the clock when Rogers sent over a cross that took a slight touch off Guehi before Watkins controlled at the far post and prodded into an empty net.

The striker celebrated in front of the Wales fans, who had just been chanting in reference to how England had lost the last two Euros finals.

Saka and Rogers tried to add another against the punch drunk visitors, who afforded the former too much space in the 20th minute.

The winger cut on to his left and made space just inside the box to bend into the top left-hand corner. Tuchel pumped his fists in celebration.

Wales looked overwhelmed. Rice rifled over a free-kick and Watkins somehow failed to add a fourth, clattering into the post as he turned an Elliot Anderson cross off target.

The striker received medical treatment and was replaced by Marcus Rashford at half-time, from which the teams returned with names removed from shirts to draw attention to memory loss.

England initially continued strongly and Rogers hit the crossbar, unaware that play would be halted for offside.

Jordan Pickford did well to stop David Brooks volleying home as Wales sought to reduce the deficit.

An even better save was to follow when denying substitute Chris Mepham's 72nd-minute header after Tuchel made four alterations, including handing Ruben Loftus-Cheek his first cap since 2018.

Both sides had half chances as the match petered out and crowd thinned as attention turned to more important World Cup qualification matters.

videos.headline: Tuchel goes in on England fans over Wembley atmosphere
videos.description: England boss Thomas Tuchel believes that atmosphere at Wembley "didn't match the performance" following his side's 3-0 win over Wales.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Presser (Tuchel goes in on England fans over Wembley atmosphere) GLOBAL 20251010

keyEvents.text: Goal! England 1, Wales 0. Morgan Rogers (England) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Marc Guéhi following a corner.
Goal! England 2, Wales 0. Ollie Watkins (England) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Marc Guéhi following a corner.
Goal! England 3, Wales 0. Bukayo Saka (England) left footed shot from the right side of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Ezri Konsa.
Neco Williams (Wales) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Marc Guéhi (England) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
First Half ends, England 3, Wales 0.
Substitution, England. Marcus Rashford replaces Ollie Watkins.
Second Half begins England 3, Wales 0.
Substitution, Wales. Jordan James replaces Harry Wilson.
Substitution, Wales. Ronan Kpakio replaces Neco Williams.
Substitution, Wales. Chris Mepham replaces Ben Davies.
Substitution, Wales. Josh Sheehan replaces Ethan Ampadu.
Substitution, England. Ruben Loftus-Cheek replaces Declan Rice.
Substitution, England. Jordan Henderson replaces Elliot Anderson.

Substitution, England. Jarrod Bowen replaces Bukayo Saka.
Djed Spence (England) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Wales. Mark Harris replaces Kieffer Moore.
Substitution, Wales. Lewis Koumas replaces Brennan Johnson.
Substitution, England. Myles Lewis-Skelly replaces John Stones.
Second Half ends, England 3, Wales 0.
